title: Infosys Expands Collaboration with ExxonMobil for Immersion Cooling in AI Infrastructure
detailed_summary: Infosys announced an expanded strategic collaboration with ExxonMobil to develop and deploy ExxonMobil™ Data Center Immersion Fluids for more energy-efficient AI and High-Performance Computing infrastructure. The collaboration uses Infosys Topaz for AI-driven cooling optimization and Infosys Cobalt for secure, scalable deployment across cloud and data center environments. Infosys stated the initiative targets hyperscalers, global enterprises, and public sector organizations in sectors including financial services, telecom, manufacturing, energy, and government, with potential outcomes including lower data center energy costs and reduced carbon emissions.

key_facts.fact: Infosys announced the expansion of its strategic collaboration with ExxonMobil to develop and deploy ExxonMobil™ Data Center Immersion Fluids.
key_facts.section_heading: Infosys Collaborates with ExxonMobil to Advance Immersion Cooling for Sustainable AI Infrastructure

key_facts.fact: The collaboration is intended to support high-efficiency cooling systems for AI and HighPerformance Computing (HPC) workloads.
key_facts.section_heading: Infosys Collaborates with ExxonMobil to Advance Immersion Cooling for Sustainable AI Infrastructure

key_facts.fact: Infosys Topaz will be used for real-time optimization of cooling operations, including smarter energy use, predictive maintenance, and scalable, reliable, and sustainable infrastructure.
key_facts.section_heading: Infosys Collaborates with ExxonMobil to Advance Immersion Cooling for Sustainable AI Infrastructure

key_facts.fact: Infosys Cobalt will provide a secure, scalable foundation for deploying the solutions across cloud and data center environments.
key_facts.section_heading: Infosys Collaborates with ExxonMobil to Advance Immersion Cooling for Sustainable AI Infrastructure

key_facts.fact: Infosys will focus on scalable solutions for hyperscalers, global enterprises, and public sector organizations across financial services, telecom, manufacturing, energy, and government.
